elements.sent-2411-001: We investigate the impact of preheating on baryogenesis in $R^2$-Higgs inflation.
elements.sent-2411-002: In this scenario, the inclusion of a dimension-six operator ${(R/ \Lambda^2) B_{\mu\nu \widetilde{B ^{\mu\nu $ abundantly generates helical hypermagnetic fields during inflation, leading to a baryon asymmetric Universe at the electroweak crossover.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-003: Focusing on the $R^2$-like regime, we first derive the relevant dynamics of preheating using a doubly-covariant formalism.
elements.sent-2411-004: We find that preheating can happen for the Higgs, transverse gauge and Goldstone bosons, however, it is dependent on the value of the non-minimal coupling $\xi_H$ between the Standard Model Higgs field and the Ricci scalar.
elements.sent-2411-005: We identify the preheating temperature to determine the appropriate scale $\Lambda$ for driving baryogenesis, which is around $\Lambda \sim 2.2 \, (2.6) \times 10^{-5}\, M_{\rm P}$ for $\xi_H \sim 1 \, (10)$.
elements.sent-2411-006: Our results represent the most accurate estimation of the scale of gravity induced baryogenesis in $R^2$-Higgs inflation to date.
elements.sent-2411-007: Areas for further improvement are identified.
elements.sent-2411-008: The existence of the baryon asymmetry of the Universe (BAU) is firmly established by various cosmological observations such as the cosmic microwave background and big-bang nucleosynthesis.
elements.sent-2411-009: However, its origin still remains unclear.
elements.sent-2411-010: If the fundamental scale of the mechanism behind the BAU is tied to a higher scale than the electroweak one, it might be possible that telltale effects at present, or even future, colliders could remain absent.
elements.sent-2411-011:
One such high-scale mechanism provides, in the Jordan frame~\cite{Cado:2023zbm , an additional source of CP violation via a (CP-odd) dimension-six operator $ {(R/ \Lambda^2) B_{\mu
u \widetilde{B ^{\mu
u $, where $R$ is the Ricci scalar and $B_{\mu
u}$ is the field stress tensor of the hypercharge $U(1)_Y$.}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-012: We will refer to this mechanism as gravity assisted baryogenesis from here onwards.
elements.sent-2411-013: In this case, even at the electroweak crossover of the Standard Model (SM), the out-of-equilibrium condition can be met if the source and washout decay rates are different and shut off at different epochs~\cite{Shaposhnikov:1987tw,Kamada:2016eeb .}
elements.sent-2411-014: The CP-violating dimension-six Chern-Simons density can abundantly produce helical hypermagnetic fields at the end of inflation~\footnote{This term, not directly connected to BAU but in the context of hypermagnetic field production, has been discussed in, e.g., Refs.~\cite{Durrer:2022emo,Durrer:2023rhc,Savchenko:2018pdr,Subramanian:2015lua,Durrer:2013pga .. .}}
elements.sent-2411-015: These helical hypermagnetic fields~\footnote{See also Refs.~\cite{Anber:2006xt,Bamba:2006km,Bamba:2007hf,Anber:2009ua,Anber:2015yca,Cado:2016kdp for the production of helical hypermagnetic fields due to inflaton dynamics.}}
elements.sent-2411-016: This mechanism can seamlessly be integrated into inflationary scenarios such as $R^2$-Higgs inflation~\cite{Salvio:2015kka,Ema:2017rqn,Pi:2017gih,Gorbunov:2018llf,Gundhi:2018wyz,He:2018mgb,Cheong:2019vzl,He:2020ivk,He:2020qcb since the ${(R/ \Lambda^2) B_{\mu\nu \widetilde{B ^{\mu\nu $ term can also be considered within the context of $f(R)$ gravity (or, rather here, $f(R,\Phi, B_\mu)$ gravity).}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-017: In the dual scalar-tensor theory, the $R^2$ term is manifest as a dynamical scalar degree of freedom, which, along with the Higgs field $\Phi$, couples to the Chern-Simons density, resulting in the production of hypermagnetic fields at the end of inflation.
elements.sent-2411-018: It should also be noted that $R^2$-Higgs inflation, like $R^2$~\cite{Starobinsky:1980te, Starobinsky:1983zz,Vilenkin:1985md,Mijic:1986iv, Maeda:1987xf inflation and Higgs inflation~\cite{Bezrukov:2007ep,Barvinsky:2008ia,Bezrukov:2010jz,Bezrukov:2013fka,DeSimone:2008ei,Bezrukov:2008ej,Barvinsky:2009ii (for similar mechanisms, see e.g.~\cite{Spokoiny:1984bd,Futamase:1987ua,Salopek:1988qh,Fakir:1990eg,Amendola:1990nn,Kaiser:1994vs,Cervantes-Cota:1995ehs,Komatsu:1999mt ), is one of the best-fit models of the Planck data~\cite{Planck:2018jri .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-019: Further, unlike Higgs inflation, where longitudinal gauge bosons are violently produced well beyond the unitarity cut-off scale~\cite{DeCross:2015uza,Ema:2016dny,Sfakianakis:2018lzf , the unitarity scale is restored up to the Planck scale in $R^2$-Higgs inflation~\cite{Ema:2017rqn .}}
elements.sent-2411-020: Thus, high-scale baryogenesis via ${(R/ \Lambda^2) B_{\mu\nu \widetilde{B ^{\mu\nu $ can be elegantly connected to the $R^2$-Higgs model without requiring additional degrees of freedom beyond the SM ones.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-021: It is not surprising that, in the gravity assisted baryogenesis, the production of hypermagnetic fields highly depends on the inflationary dynamics.
elements.sent-2411-022: The baryon-to-entropy ratio is also highly sensitive to the value of the magnetic Reynolds number which in turn highly depends on the exact (p)reheating temperature~\cite{Kamada:2016cnb,Cado:2021bia,Cado:2022evn,Cado:2022pxk,Cado:2023zbm .}
elements.sent-2411-023: In addition, the latter is also highly dependent on the value of quantum hypermagnetic energy density when preheating is complete.
elements.sent-2411-024: Therefore, the exact amount of baryon asymmetry will be model-dependent as it makes use of specific (p)reheating results~\footnote{In the absence of efficient preheating, thermalization proceeds through perturbative reheating.. .}
elements.sent-2411-025: An improved understanding of these dynamics brings about major improvements in the precision of predictions since we do not need to rely on estimates as in previous studies but directly determine the thermal plasma temperature, the relevant energy densities and the scale factor ratio at reheating and the end of inflation.
elements.sent-2411-026: Previous studies performed by the current authors treated such effects as effective parameters~\cite{Cado:2023zbm , while a detailed analysis of how (p)reheating impacts gravity assisted baryogenesis was missing.}
elements.sent-2411-027: This will entail us to find a more precise value for the $\Lambda$ required for baryogenesis.
elements.sent-2411-028: In this work, taking $R^2$-Higgs inflation as a benchmark model for inflation, and focusing primarily on the $R^2$-like regime, we study the impact of preheating and particle production in the mechanism of gravity assisted baryogenesis.
elements.sent-2411-029: Adopting the doubly-covariant formalism and including all perturbations at the linear order, we study the preheating dynamics of the scalar and gauge sectors.
elements.sent-2411-030: In Ref.~\cite{Sfakianakis:2018lzf , Sfakianakis and van de Vis did provide a similar detailed analysis of linear fluctuations during preheating in Higgs inflation in the Einstein frame.}
elements.sent-2411-031: In our case, the field space of the inflationary dynamics consists of five fields: one dynamical scalar degree of freedom arising due to the $R^2$ term and four from the Higgs $SU(2)_L$ doublet, corresponding to the physical Higgs and the three Goldstone bosons.
elements.sent-2411-032: The background dynamics are governed by the $R^2$ scalar alongside the physical Higgs boson; the three would-be Goldstone bosons are treated as perturbations.
elements.sent-2411-033: Our results include the full $SU(2)_L\times U(1)_Y$ gauge dynamics in a complete analysis.
elements.sent-2411-034: The inflaton's self-resonance turns out to not be efficient enough for preheating.
elements.sent-2411-035: However, Higgs fluctuations can lead to preheating if the non-minimal coupling $\xi_H$ between the Ricci scalar $R$ and the Higgs is $\gtrsim 10$.
elements.sent-2411-036: We also show that for $\xi_H \sim 10$, the transverse modes of the $Z$ and $W$ boson can lead to gauge preheating.
elements.sent-2411-037: For tiny $\xi_H$ values, gauge preheating might be induced if $\Lambda$ is sufficiently small.
elements.sent-2411-038: We shall see that such a small value of $\Lambda$ would, however, lead to an overproduction of baryon asymmetry.
elements.sent-2411-039: Further, Goldstone bosons may also preheat the Universe even for $\xi_H\sim 1$ (see also Refs.~\cite{Ema:2016dny,Bezrukov:2019ylq,Garcia-Bellido:2008ycs,He:2020ivk,Bezrukov:2020txg,Figueroa:2021iwm,Jeong:2023zrv for Goldstone/longitudinal gauge boson preheating in $R^2$-Higgs inflation).}
elements.sent-2411-040: Our results show that the Goldstone bosons can preheat the Universe mildly faster than any other fields and, hence, they determine the reheating temperature needed for the baryogenesis computation.
elements.sent-2411-041: We organize this paper as follows.
elements.sent-2411-042: First, we start with outlining the action and derive the relevant equations of motion (EoM) for the different fields in Sec.~\ref{sec:action .}
elements.sent-2411-043: We discuss the inflationary dynamics in the covariant formalism in Sec.~\ref{sec:infdynamics .}
elements.sent-2411-044: The production of inflaton and Higgs fluctuations are studied in Sec.~\ref{sec:Inf+Higgs-fluctuations , and the production of the $Z$, $W$ and Goldstone bosons is presented in Sec.~\ref{sec:GWZ-production .}}
elements.sent-2411-045: The production of hypermagnetic fields and subsequent generation of the BAU are discussed, respectively, in Sec.~\ref{sec:EM-production and Sec.~\ref{sec:baryogensis .}}
elements.sent-2411-047: Finally, we summarize and conclude in Sec.~\ref{sec:disc .}
elements.sent-2411-048: Some technical and computational details are relegated to App.~\ref{app:gaugegoldspectra .}

elements.sent-2411-295: Nonetheless, these nonlinear effects may indeed dampen the strength of preheating and, in some cases may completely shut down preheating for certain species.

elements.sent-2411-297: Let us begin with the decay of the produced particles.

elements.sent-2411-299: The produced Higgs as well as the Goldstone particles may decay into SM fermions and gauge bosons.

elements.sent-2411-382: In this work, we studied the implications of the preheating on gravity assisted baryogenesis in $R^2$-Higgs inflation, namely how preheating can impact on baryogenesis at the electroweak crossover from the production of helical hypermagnetic fields.
elements.sent-2411-383: To this end, we adopted the doubly-covariant formalism for both inflationary dynamics and gauge field production.
elements.sent-2411-384: We derived the equations of motion and energy densities for the inflaton, Higgs background fields, and relevant perturbations at linear order.
elements.sent-2411-385: This includes the inflationary fields, the $W^\pm$, $Z$ bosons, the photon, and the three Goldstone fields.
elements.sent-2411-386: The Coulomb gauge was used, as the unitary gauge becomes ill-defined at Higgs zero-crossings.
elements.sent-2411-387: Hence, the Goldstone bosons remained dynamical in our discussion.
elements.sent-2411-388: The preheating is governed by the field-space manifold, the dynamics of the background condensates, the respective effective masses and the coupled metric perturbations.
elements.sent-2411-389: We primarily focused on $R^2$ and mildly mixed $R^2$-Higgs-like regimes, however expressions and the formalism can be applied to other regimes of $\xi_R$ and $\xi_H$.
elements.sent-2411-390: We highlight different phenomenological possibilities by identifying three benchmark points: a deep $R^2$-like scenario with $\xi_H \approx 10^{-3}$ (BP$a$), $\xi_H \approx 1$ (BP$b$) and a mixed $R^2$-Higgs scenario with $\xi_H \approx 10$ (BP$c$) (see Tab.~\ref{parmeterchoices ).}
elements.sent-2411-391: We find that the Higgs quanta and transverse modes of the $W$ boson can preheat the Universe for BP$c$ for $\xi_H \approx 10$, while the $Z$ boson can provide successful preheating for both BP$b$ and BP$c$.
elements.sent-2411-392: The Goldstone sector can also preheat for BP$b$ and BP$c$.
elements.sent-2411-393: We find that for both BP$b$ and BP$c$, the Goldstone bosons can preheat the Universe faster than any other field: at $\mathcal N \approx \,3 \,$ and $\mathcal N \approx \,1.8 \,$, respectively (see Fig.~\ref{plot:rhoG ).}
elements.sent-2411-394: In all cases, preheating never happens for BP$a$ unless $\Lambda$ is small.
elements.sent-2411-395: We remark that our results for preheating are in good agreement with previous studies in this model~\cite{He:2018mgb,He:2020ivk , however, we find that $\xi_H \approx 1$ is also sufficient for preheating of the $\phi_3$ field at $\mathcal N \approx \,3$.}

elements.sent-2411-397: We find that the value of $\Lambda$, required for baryogenesis does depend on $\xi_H$ indirectly via the reheating temperature.
elements.sent-2411-398: We identify a window around $\Lambda \sim 2.2 \, (2.6) \times 10^{-5}\, M_{\rm P}$ for $\xi_H \approx 1 \, (10)$ where the observed baryon asymmetry of the Universe can be achieved.
elements.sent-2411-399: As preheating happens earlier for larger $\xi_H$, leading to larger reheating temperature, a larger value of $\Lambda$ is required for successful baryogenesis.
elements.sent-2411-400: However, our analysis also reveals that sufficiently small values of $\Lambda$ could lead to gauge preheating as found in Fig.~\ref{plot:WZenergydensity and Fig.~\ref{plot:rhoA-All-Lambda for BP$a$.}}
elements.sent-2411-401: Nevertheless, based on earlier findings from Ref.~\cite{Cado:2023zbm and pending further investigation into fermion-gauge boson backreaction, we expect the inclusion of the Schwinger effect to significantly impact our results.}
elements.sent-2411-402: Importantly, incorporating the Schwinger effect is unlikely to alter the successful sourcing of baryon number density (see also Refs.~\cite{Cado:2022pxk, Cado:2023zbm ).}
elements.sent-2411-403: Moreover, the relevance of the Schwinger effect dramatically depends on the value of the fermion masses at high values of the background Higgs field, and so on the mechanism for the generation of fermion masses at high scales.
elements.sent-2411-404: For instance a particular Froggatt-Nielsen mechanism was presented in Ref.~\cite{Cado:2023gan where there is no Schwinger effect for the Standard Model at inflationary scales, while reproducing the fermion spectrum at electroweak scales.}
elements.sent-2411-405: In that case, small values of $\Lambda$ are disfavored as they tend to overproduce the baryon asymmetry.
elements.sent-2411-406: Our work systematically builds upon previous studies~\cite{Cado:2022evn,Cado:2023gan,Cado:2023zbm by explicitly computing, for the first time, the reheating time, temperature, and energy without taking them as effective parameters in the model.}
elements.sent-2411-407: One remaining uncertainty regarding the baryogenesis mechanism, which we leave for future work, concerns the specific dynamics of the electroweak crossover, particularly the evolution of the weak mixing angle from zero to its low-energy value.
elements.sent-2411-408: In the present analysis, we ensured that the helicity generated during reheating is preserved until the electroweak scale by carefully considering the plasma's Reynolds number and verifying that the chiral plasma instability does not impact our results.
elements.sent-2411-409: We also remark that for our preheating dynamics, we adopted a naive effective approach related to how decays impact the produced quanta instead of incorporating them in the EoMs directly.
